stories.title: HMRC Annual Report and Accounts 2025 to 2026 Published
stories.summary: HMRC has published its annual report and accounts for 2025–2026. Highlights include a 64% rise in Self Assessment payments via the HMRC app in January 2026, and Budget 2025 measures expected to raise an additional £845 million in tax revenue by 2030–31 through improved data use.

stories.title: HMRC External Commitments: Supplementary Note Released
stories.summary: HMRC has released a supplementary note on its external commitments alongside the annual report, detailing Budget 2025 measures that will raise £845 million in additional tax revenues through improved data analytics by 2030–31.

stories.title: Statement of Changes to Immigration Rules Published (HC 259, 9 July 2026)
stories.summary: The Home Secretary has laid new changes to the Immigration Rules before Parliament (HC 259), updating the practice to be followed in the administration of immigration and visas. This is one of several statements of changes issued throughout the year.

stories.title: Annual Statistics on Scientific Procedures on Living Animals 2025 Published
stories.summary: The Home Office has published its annual statistical report on regulated scientific procedures involving living animals in Great Britain for 2025, covering the number of procedures and types of research conducted.

stories.title: FCDO Updates Nigeria Travel Advice — Against Travel to Kwara and Benue States
stories.summary: The FCDO has updated its Nigeria travel advice, now advising against all but essential travel to Kwara State and Benue State, reflecting escalating security concerns in those regions.

stories.title: Saudi Arabia: Gulf Strategy Fund Call for Concept Notes 2026–2027
stories.summary: The British Embassy in Riyadh has issued a call for concept notes under the Gulf Strategy Fund, with projects funded in the range of £60,000–£80,000 to be delivered by 30 November 2026. Deadline for concept notes is 17 July 2026.

stories.title: DWP Annual Report and Accounts 2025 to 2026 Published
stories.summary: The Department for Work and Pensions has published its annual report covering performance and expenditure from 1 April 2025 to 31 March 2026, providing a comprehensive overview of its benefits and pensions activities.

stories.title: Timms Review of Personal Independence Payment (PIP): Interim Report Published
stories.summary: An interim report from the Timms Review into PIP has been published, revealing DWP forecasts that the number of PIP/DLA recipients may more than double to over 4.4 million people by 2031, up from levels seen in 2020. The report examines eligibility, assessment processes, and future costs.

stories.title: Air Quality Alert Bulletin Issued — 9 July 2026
stories.summary: DEFRA's UK AIR service has issued an Air Quality Information and Alert Bulletin for 9 July 2026, flagging elevated pollution levels across parts of England.

stories.title: Capital Grant Scheme Reopened with £150 Million to Support Nature-Friendly Farming
stories.summary: The Secretary of State for DEFRA announced the reopening of the Capital Grant scheme with £150 million in funding at the Groundswell Show on 3 July, aimed at incentivising nature-friendly farming practices across England.

stories.title: Rural Payments Agency Annual Report and Accounts 2025 to 2026 Published
stories.summary: The Rural Payments Agency (RPA), an executive agency of DEFRA, has published its annual report and accounts for 2025–2026, covering direct aid and rural development payments to English farmers.

stories.title: US Plans to Spend $500m on RAF Fairford Raise Concerns in Parliament
stories.summary: An MP has raised questions with the UK government about reported US plans to invest $500 million (£373 million) in expansion and military infrastructure at RAF Fairford in Gloucestershire, seeking clarity on the nature and implications of these plans.

stories.title: New Science-Led Training Guide Launched to Improve Servicewomen's Health
stories.summary: The MoD has launched a new science-led performance guide for Servicewomen to transform how they train, fuel and recover for demanding military roles, marking a significant step toward inclusive fitness standards.

stories.title: Location Statistics for UK Regular Armed Forces and Civilians 2026 Published
stories.summary: The MoD has published its 2026 location statistics covering the national and international postings of all UK regular armed forces personnel and MoD civilian staff.

stories.title: UK Life Sciences Sector Attracts £3 Billion Investment
stories.summary: The Department for Business and Trade announced on 9 July 2026 that the UK life sciences sector has secured £3 billion in new investment, expected to create jobs and deliver faster patient treatments across the country.

stories.title: Fair Work Agency Framework Agreement Published
stories.summary: DBT and the Fair Work Agency have published their framework agreement, approved by HM Treasury, setting out the operational and governance arrangements between the two bodies.

stories.title: Report: £multi-billion Economic Opportunity in UK Climate Adaptation Products and Services
stories.summary: DBT has published a report identifying a significant economic opportunity for UK businesses in climate adaptation, estimating a large global market for British products and services that help people and organisations manage climate risks.

agencies.name: DSIT
agencies.description: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

stories.title: Intellectual Property Office Innovation and Growth Report 2025/26 Published
stories.summary: The IPO, an executive agency of DSIT, has published its Innovation and Growth Report for 2025/26, highlighting the role of IP rights in supporting UK innovation and economic growth.

stories.title: IPO Annual Report and Accounts 2025 to 2026 Published
stories.summary: The Intellectual Property Office, sponsored by DSIT, has published its annual report and accounts for 2025–2026, providing a full picture of its performance, operations, and financials.

stories.title: NHS A&Es Face 'Summer Onslaught' with Busiest June on Record
stories.summary: NHS England has warned that A&E departments are experiencing their busiest month on record in June, driven by record heatwaves and increased footfall linked to the World Cup. The NHS is urging the public to use 111 and pharmacies to relieve pressure on emergency services.

stories.title: South East Patients Report Higher Satisfaction with GP Access
stories.summary: NHS England (South East) reports that patients in the region are happier with their experience of general practice, as access continues to improve following investments in GP appointments and NHS facilities announced earlier this week.

stories.title: Government's 10-Year Capital Plan to Boost GP Infrastructure
stories.summary: The Government's 10 Year Capital Plan announced this week will allow GP practices across England to convert unused rooms into consulting space and modernise facilities, improving patient care and access nationwide.

stories.title: Royal Mail's Failure to Meet Universal Service Obligation Raised in Parliament
stories.summary: An urgent question was raised in the House of Commons regarding Royal Mail's failure to meet its universal service obligation, putting pressure on the government to act on delivery reliability and network performance.

stories.title: Surrey Town Set to Lose 'Crucial' Central Post Office
stories.summary: A town in Surrey is facing the closure of its central Post Office branch, which residents and local businesses have described as a crucial community resource. The closure is part of an ongoing pattern of Post Office branch reductions across the UK.
